title: AMS adopts the resonance/comparative essay convention from klappy.dev
content: AMS adopts the resonance/comparative essay convention from klappy.dev. First entry: canon/resonance/recursive-mas.md, documenting the relationship between AMS and Yang et al., "Recursive Multi-Agent Systems," arXiv:2604.25917 (28 April 2026). The convention requires every cited work to include at least one explicit divergence; without divergence, the citation does not belong. Files are named after the work, not the principle. Voice is neutral. Sections are fixed: AMS Principle, Convergent Quotes (non-authoritative, max 3), Where AMS Aligns, Where AMS Diverges (Explicit, mandatory), Why the Divergence Matters, Operationalization in AMS, Related Canon. The directory canon/resonance/ is created by this commit; future resonance pages slot in as siblings.

title: RecursiveMAS appeared the day after the AMS hackathon
content: RecursiveMAS was published on arXiv on 28 April 2026, the day after the hackathon that originated AMS. The team read the paper the next day; the comparison against AMS surfaced on 2 May 2026. Two parallel attempts to solve the same diagnosis (text-shaped messaging is wrong for agents) with mirror-image remedies (collapse the wire into the model versus keep the wire dumber than the model) arrived inside the same week. The contemporaneity matters: RecursiveMAS is not a precursor that AMS rejects, and it is not a successor that supersedes AMS. The two designs are live alternatives to each other, and the resonance page is the artifact that makes the live disagreement legible.

title: MD indexes are a lie in wait
content: Markdown index files drift out of sync with the directories they enumerate. They claim membership but encode a moment: the directory grows, the index stays, and the gap between the two becomes a quiet source of false confidence. The promise of an index is "these are the contents"; the reality after one missed update is "these were the contents on the day someone wrote this file." AMS deliberately does not bootstrap canon/resonance/README.md alongside the first resonance page. Discoverability lives in canon search and in cross-reference between pages, not in a static index that has to be hand-maintained. Open question: whether this generalizes into a constraint against directory-level README index files anywhere in canon, or remains a local choice for the resonance directory.

title: Resonance pages are not essays; they are mechanical comparisons
content: A resonance page is not an essay or a synthesis or a citation. It is a mechanical comparison: the host project's principle stated first in its own terms, then alignment described concretely, then divergence stated explicitly. The template's "if this section feels uncomfortable, that is a signal the citation is weak" is load-bearing. The first draft of the RecursiveMAS comparison was written in the AMS first-person essay voice (matching ESSAY.md), which is the wrong voice for canon. The polished version uses the neutral declarative voice that AMS canon documents already follow (D0001, permanent-non-goals). Voice mixing is a tell that the form was not respected. Resonance pages are also subject to the same AI-voice-cliché constraints as any public-facing canon: em-dash discipline, no negation parallelism, no formulaic transitions, no generic puffery.

title: The 2025–2026 latent-communication cluster
content: RecursiveMAS belongs to a research cluster pushing toward richer model-internal channels between agents: Du et al. on latent-space communication, Fu et al. on cache-to-cache semantic transfer, Zheng et al. on thought communication, Zou et al. on latent collaboration in multi-agent systems. All four works share the premise that closer coupling between agents yields meaningful efficiency and accuracy gains inside controlled systems. The cluster is correct about this for closed systems. AMS occupies the position the cluster vacates: the substrate that has to exist underneath any of those systems if any two of them are to coordinate across an organizational boundary, host an observer outside the gradient path, support asynchronous wake-up, or substitute one agent for another without retraining. The cluster is the alternative future AMS implicitly takes a position against. Future resonance pages may cover individual cluster members; the current page treats the cluster as one alternative posture and names its members in passing.

title: Resonance directory bootstrapped at canon/resonance/
content: canon/resonance/recursive-mas.md committed. Directory canon/resonance/ now exists. No README index. Cross-references from related canon: D0001 (tokens-not-messages), permanent-non-goals, vodka-architecture-applied, operator-as-subscriber, observability-as-subscriber, D0009 (stream-as-primitive-ownership-excludes-subscription), ESSAY.md. Convention source: klappy://canon/resonance.
